about--docs.name: Docs
about--docs.title: About
about--docs.content: # EOxElements [beta] {/* prettier-ignore */} GitHub repository ## Introduction EOxElements are a set of reusable, modular and highly configurable components for building geospatial applications. The goal is to reduce code duplication during development, and offer ready-made solutions for quick bootstrapping and increased long-term stabilty. ## Technology The EOxElements are a suite of custom elements (a.k.a. [web components](https://www.webcomponents.org/introduction)), which are framework-agnostic. Thus, they can be used in applications built with Vanilla JavaScript, Vue.js, React, Angular etc. For usage examples please have a look at [the provided examples](/docs/usage--docs). ## Jupyter Wrapper Additionally to being usable in JavaScript, there is also a wrapper for Jupyter notebooks, called [EOxElements-Jupyter](https://github.com/EOX-A/EOxElements-Jupyter). ## Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server For usage with AI coding agents, EOxElements provides a server that exposes a set of tools via the Model Context Protocol. For more information, see the [MCP documentation](/docs/mcp--docs).

coding-agents--docs.name: Docs
coding-agents--docs.title: Coding Agents
coding-agents--docs.content:
# Coding Agents

EOxElements provides several resources to help AI coding agents understand and work with the library.

## LLMs.txt

We provide structured documentation files specifically designed for Large Language Models (LLMs). These files contain comprehensive information about the library, making it easy for AI tools to understand and assist with EOxElements development.

Available routes:

- [`/llms.txt`](./llms.txt) - Contains a structured overview of all documentation pages and their links.
- [`/llms-full.txt`](./llms-full.txt) - Provides comprehensive documentation including getting started guides, API references, and usage examples (snippets).

## MCP Server

The EOxElements MCP Server provides a set of tools for a coding agent to get information about the EOxElements custom elements. It uses the [@modelcontextprotocol/sdk](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk) to expose the tools.

### Hosted

To use the MCP server without installing anything locally, you can point your coding agent to our hosted endpoint:

`https://elements.mcp.eox.at`

### Install

#### NPX

You can use npx to run the mcp-server:

```bash
npx @eox/elements-mcp-server
```

#### Global Install

Install the package globally from npm:

```bash
npm install -g @eox/elements-mcp-server
```

Then, run the server:

```bash
eox-elements-mcp-server
```

### Getting Started

Every agentic AI software is different, but usually some config file needs to be set up to define the available MCP servers.
The `@eox/elements-mcp-server` will start on `http://localhost:3000` (hosted: `https://elements.mcp.eox.at`) and a compatible coding agent will be able to discover and use the tools from that endpoint.

### Available Tools

The following tools are available:

- `list_elements`: List all available EOxElements custom elements.
- `get_element_details`: Get the full details for a specific EOxElements custom element.
- `get_element_stories`: Get the stories (examples/snippets) for a specific EOxElements custom element.
- `get_element_attributes`: Get the attributes for a specific EOxElements custom element.
- `get_element_properties`: Get the properties for a specific EOxElements custom element.
- `get_element_events`: Get the events for a specific EOxElements custom element.
- `get_element_methods`: Get the methods for a specific EOxElements custom element.
- `get_element_slots`: Get the slots for a specific EOxElements custom element.
- `get_element_css_properties`: Get the CSS custom properties for a specific EOxElements custom element.
- `get_element_css_parts`: Get the CSS shadow parts for a specific EOxElements custom element.
